queue_signal.headline_snippet: Muzaffarpur Hospital ICU Fire: IMA demands revocation of hospital licence suspension - Medical Dialogues

record_snapshot.title: Muzaffarpur Hospital Fire
record_snapshot.note: Copied the current case record before editing.

duplicate_guard.reason: no duplicate found; this is a distinct follow-up accountability update for the same incident

identity_gate.notes: The frozen Medical Dialogues article describes the same Muzaffarpur Prasad Hospital fire and the regulatory response to it; it adds an IMA protest over the licence suspension rather than a different incident.
supporting_quotes: Muzaffarpur Hospital ICU Fire: IMA demands revocation of hospital licence suspension
Following the suspension of Prasad Hospital's registration and the arrest of the three staff members after a fire claimed six lives

changes.value: A fire broke out in the ICU of Prasad Hospital in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, on 4 June 2026. Later reporting said the death toll rose to six ICU patients, that three staff members — doctor Pankaj, admin manager Ram Kumar, and maintenance manager Ajit Kumar — were arrested, that the Bihar government suspended the hospital licence, that Telegraph India later reported the hospital's registration was cancelled after the fire, that 13 health facilities were sealed in Muzaffarpur after the fire, that Ram Kripal Yadav demanded a probe into the hospital's fire safety standards, and that Medical Dialogues reported the IMA Muzaffarpur demanded immediate revocation of the hospital-registration suspension and withdrawal of action against the ICU doctor.
changes.exact_quote: The medical body also sought the withdrawal of administrative action against the doctor in-charge of the ICU, and warned of agitation if its demands were not met.

value: The Telegraph India fallback headline says the hospital registration was cancelled after the fire and renders the death count as seven, while the source URL slug says kills six and the case record still supports 6 deaths, so the record keeps deaths: 6.

Medical Dialogues' article is internally inconsistent on fatalities: the intro says the fire claimed six lives, while a later paragraph says seven lives. This update does not change the existing deaths count.
changes.exact_quote: Following the fire incident at the hospital, which claimed seven lives, police arrested three staff members on Friday for negligence.

value.event: Medical Dialogues reported that the IMA Muzaffarpur termed the hospital-registration suspension unconstitutional and demanded its immediate revocation, along with withdrawal of administrative action against the ICU doctor.

changes.exact_quote: "The civil surgeon's decision to suspend the hospital's registration without seeking an explanation from the management is unconstitutional. We demand its immediate reversal,"

conflict_notes: The Telegraph India fallback headline says the hospital registration was cancelled after the fire and renders the death count as seven, while the source URL slug says kills six and the case record still supports 6 deaths, so the record keeps deaths: 6.

Medical Dialogues' article is internally inconsistent on fatalities: the intro says the fire claimed six lives, while a later paragraph says seven lives. This update does not change the existing deaths count.

draft_diff.notes: Added the June 9 IMA revocation update, preserved the existing fatality count, and recorded the Medical Dialogues internal inconsistency as a conflict instead of inferring a new death total.

critic_review.notes: All populated claims are directly supported by the frozen Medical Dialogues text or existing frozen case sources. The new entry stays within the same Muzaffarpur hospital fire incident family.

validation.notes: Frontmatter parses cleanly, the Medical Dialogues source is specific and frozen, the Hugo build succeeded, and the new entry is a distinct follow-up update rather than a duplicate incident record.
